blocks.body: Reflexive pronouns are pronouns ending in -self (singular) or -selves (plural). They show that the subject and the object of the action are the same person: I taught myself, She cut herself, They enjoyed themselves.

{ "title": "8. Brief summary", "items": [ "Reflexive pronouns: myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves.", "Form: -self (singular), -selves (plural).", "Reflexive use: when subject = object (action on myself): I hurt myself.", "Emphatic use: for reinforcement (by myself): I did it myself.", "By myself = independently or alone.", "For myself = for myself.", "Some verbs DO NOT need a reflexive: feel, meet, get dressed, wash, shave.", "Each other = each other (mutually). Reflexive = self." ] }
